mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly reduce expected corporate revenues and consumer-driven earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market prices in weaker aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales fell sharply in late 2008 following the September 2008 financial shock
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is declining month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 0.5%, the zero lower bound eliminates conventional monetary policy space and triggers expectations of unconventional easing, compressing the equity risk premium and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns as liquidity provisions expand.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve lowered the target rate to 0-0.25% in December 2008
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and declines in industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a synchronized deterioration across labor and supply channels, amplifying downward earnings revisions and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns that are larger than either signal alone.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rose to 7.2% in December 2008 while industrial production posted record declines
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is rising month-over-month and INDPRO is falling month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns only when the economy is already in a high-unemployment recession, because in that regime deflationary dynamics reflect collapsing demand rather than positive supply shocks.
evidence_refs: fact:Headline inflation turned negative in late 2008 with unemployment above 7%
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and CPIAUCSL is falling month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index (INDPRO) signal a sustained manufacturing contraction that reduces capacity utilization and corporate margins, leading to negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are marked down.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production fell in both October and November 2008
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of episodes following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal a double-drag on household wealth and consumption, amplifying the negative wealth effect and generating negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns as the market prices in a prolonged deleveraging cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts remained near historic lows in late 2008 while unemployment climbed rapidly
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below its 12-month moving average and UNRATE is rising month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reductions lower the discount rate applied to corporate cash flows; when FEDFUNDS sits below its trailing 6-month average, the cost of capital is compressed and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns as the market reprices equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate was cut from 5.25% to 0-0.25% between September 2007 and December 2008
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below its 6-month trailing average.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contractions in real GDP (GDPC1) confirm an official recession, triggering systematic de-risking by institutional investors and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns as the negative growth surprise is digested by the market.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP contracted in Q3 2008 and the advance Q4 2008 release was anticipated to show a severe decline
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns following a negative GDP print in more than 45% of observations.
